danger_signals.meaning: Opponent vulnerable to pinfall or further attacks; critical window for match advantage

danger_signals.visual_form: Referee on stomach performing three-count gesture near pinned opponent
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent loss if three-count completes; player must break hold or escape

danger_signals.visual_form: Wrestler sprite positioned beyond ring boundary
danger_signals.meaning: Risk of count-out loss if wrestler doesn't re-enter before 20-count completes

danger_signals.visual_form: Match progression UI showing defensive record (e.g., '3 of 10 defenses')
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating pressure: must complete 10 consecutive title defenses to advance to final boss

safety_signals.visual_form: Full-height sprite facing opponent in neutral stance
safety_signals.meaning: Safe position; ready to engage or execute attacks

safety_signals.visual_form: Defined rectangular canvas area with rope boundaries
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; no count-out risk while present; referee authority zone

safety_signals.visual_form: Wrestler sprite breaks from prone position and stands
Visual status indicator in championship mode
safety_signals.meaning: High prestige status; unlocks access to title defense sequence

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: VWA logo centered in ring (US/European version; FWA logo in Japanese version)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: All CPU opponents represent VWA roster members
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ring centerpiece logo; championship belt visual; VWA Champion designation for title holder

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: VWF logo/championship designation associated with Great Puma
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Great Puma appears as inter-promotional rival with distinct visual treatment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Special logo used in Great Puma match; appears only as final boss after 10 VWA title defenses

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: King Slender as default VWA Champion; if player selects King Slender, Giant Panther becomes champion substitute
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: King Slender appears as first major milestone opponent (after 5 initial matches); some game versions have bug requiring extra victories if player selects him

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Incrementally difficult CPU opponents; character select screen with portrait gallery; ring centerpiece displays VWA logo
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: CPU opponent difficulty increases; opponent AI execution of special moves becomes more frequent and complex

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: King Slender selected as opponent; championship belt becomes visual focus; special match designation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: King Slender executes advanced moves; higher damage output; more aggressive pinfall attempts

world_state_to_visual.world_state: VWA Champion status (player has won title)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UI displays title defense counter ('X of 10 defenses'); championship belt visual accompanies player; VWA Champion designation in match intros
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: CPU opponents cycle through all five roster members; difficulty remains high; opponent selection predetermined in sequence

world_state_to_visual.world_state: VWF Championship match unlocked (after 10 title defenses)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Great Puma appears as final opponent; special championship logo replaces standard VWA ring logo; inter-promotional title designation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Great Puma executes most complex move set; highest damage output; match deemed by community as one of NES' most difficult boss encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character select prevents duplicate selection; ring lacks championship context; no time limit display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Match format changes to best-of-three-falls (vs. single fall in single-player); no five-minute timer; player-vs-player control removes CPU behavior patterns

environmental_storytelling.example: FWA logo in Japanese Disk System version; VWA logo in North American/European versions and FamicomBox. Signals regional wrestling association identity and indicates localization scope.
Belt appears in championship mode UI and winner screens; indicates prestige tier and career progression milestone reached.
Referee must physically run across ring to reach pinned wrestlers; distance from pin location determines time window for escape. Communicates game physics authenticity and adds spatial tension.
Game features cameraman and commentators at ringside (non-interactive). Establishes professional wrestling broadcast context without mechanically integrating crowd feedback.

Character names and origins (Fighter Hayabusa—Japan, Giant Panther—USA, Kin Corn Karn—Korea, Starman—Mexico, The Amazon—Parts Unknown, King Slender—USA) establish international wrestling federation narrative without explicit faction mechanics.
Title defense counter ('3 of 10') visually communicates escalating pressure and number of remaining gate matches before final boss access.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Basic move execution; moderate pinfall attempts; predictable lock-up patterns; lower damage output
Advanced special move execution; frequent pinfall attempts; higher damage per move; aggressive ring control; appears after 5 initial wins
Increased move complexity; higher damage threshold; more frequent special move usage; opponents cycle through all five roster members sequentially

Most complex move set in game; maximum damage output per attack; high-frequency special move execution; described as one of NES' most difficult boss encounters; appears only after 10 successful title defenses
Prone on canvas; susceptible to pinfall; can be hauled upright by opponent (resetting vulnerability window but granting brief counter window)
Physically relocates to pinned wrestlers; lies on stomach; performs three-count gesture; count timing depends on referee proximity (spatial delay mechanic)

notes: Pro Wrestling (1987 NES) uses minimal explicit faction systems but establishes implicit wrestling promotion hierarchy through UI state progression. VWA/VWF logo distinction signals inter-promotional narrative. Danger/safety signals rely heavily on spatial ring positioning (in-ring vs. out-of-ring) and match state progression (championship vs. non-championship). The game's manifestation layer is primarily behavioral (difficulty escalation, move set complexity) rather than visual thematic variation. Character roster geographic origins provide implicit cultural/regional context without mechanically integrated faction mechanics. Referee positioning introduces spatial tension as a core danger signal. Regional localization (FWA→VWA logo change) reflects commercial/naming adaptation rather than world-state changes.
